concepts.finding: Annoy uses random projection trees to partition high-dimensional space, building a forest of trees where each node splits with a random hyperplane, enabling fast ANN with memory-mapped indexes.
concepts.implication: Tree-based ANN with random projections provides memory-efficient, easily indexable approach for static datasets, widely used in production for item recommendation and similarity search.
concepts.open_question: How does Annoy compare with HNSW for dynamic datasets where items are frequently added or removed?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Annoy will have comparable recall to HNSW on static datasets but 3x+ faster index rebuild times, making it preferable for periodically refreshed search indexes.
